skill.name: memory-bank
skill.description: Persistent project documentation system that maintains structured project context across assistant sessions.
skill.summary: Create and maintain Memory Bank files so project context, decisions, and progress survive across sessions.
skill.author: 0xkynz

security_audit.summary: The static external-command findings are mostly false positives from Markdown fences, and the weak-crypto and reconnaissance detections have no supporting code evidence. The meaningful risks are semantic: the skill is always applied, directs broad trust in persistent Memory Bank files, and expects filesystem writes.

medium_findings.title: Always-On Persistent Memory Trust
medium_findings.description: The skill is configured for automatic application and instructs the assistant to rely entirely on Memory Bank files. If those files are stale or poisoned, future sessions may inherit unsafe instructions or incorrect project state.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The alwaysApply flag and mandatory memory-loading instructions are explicit. The risk depends on future Memory Bank content, so this is a medium semantic risk rather than confirmed malicious behavior.

medium_findings.title: Expected Filesystem Modification
medium_findings.description: The workflow directs the assistant to create and update project documentation files. This is legitimate for the skill, but users should expect repository changes when it is active.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file creation and update behavior is directly described. It is not malicious, but it changes project files and therefore deserves a marketplace warning.

low_findings.title: Static Command Findings Are Mostly Markdown Fences
low_findings.description: The reported Ruby backtick execution locations are Markdown code fences and documentation examples. No evidence found of Ruby execution, dynamic command construction, or automatic command execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited areas are fenced Markdown blocks or templates. The only actual shell command is a simple directory creation example at lines 133-135.

low_findings.title: Weak-Crypto And Reconnaissance Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: No evidence found of cryptographic code or network reconnaissance behavior. The cited lines contain prose, Markdown filenames, placeholders, or checklist text.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The source is documentation-only Markdown with no cryptographic API calls or networking commands. The flagged lines do not implement executable behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Illustrative Shell Command
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill includes a bash example that creates the memory-bank directory. The command is static and low risk, but it is still an external command a user may execute.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The command is present and executable if copied or followed. It is hardcoded and limited to directory creation, so the security impact is low.

content.value_statement: Long-running projects lose context when assistant sessions reset. This skill creates a structured Memory Bank so Claude, Codex, and Claude Code can resume work with project history.
actual_capabilities: Defines a required memory-bank folder with six core Markdown files.
Explains the order for reading project context at session start.
Provides templates for project brief, product context, active context, system patterns, technical context, and progress.
Guides updates after major decisions, implementation work, and user requests.
Supports additional documentation files for complex features, APIs, tests, and deployments.
limitations: It does not provide scripts that automatically validate Memory Bank contents.
It relies on the assistant to keep documentation accurate and current.
It can preserve outdated or unsafe instructions if users do not review stored files.
It is documentation-focused and does not replace source control or issue tracking.

use_cases.title: Maintain Context For Solo Development
use_cases.description: Keep project goals, current work, decisions, and progress available between assistant sessions.

use_cases.title: Document Architecture During Iteration
use_cases.description: Record system patterns, technical constraints, and design decisions while features evolve.

use_cases.title: Prepare Handoffs For AI-Assisted Work
use_cases.description: Give future Claude, Codex, or Claude Code sessions a structured starting point for continuing work.

prompt_templates.title: Initialize Memory Bank
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new project
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a memory-bank folder for this project. Ask me for the project brief details first, then draft the required Memory Bank files.

prompt_templates.title: Resume From Memory Bank
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the Memory Bank files in order and summarize the project state, current focus, blockers, and next recommended steps.

prompt_templates.title: Update After Work
prompt_templates.prompt: Update activeContext.md and progress.md to reflect the work completed in this session, the current state, and immediate next steps.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Project Memory
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review before a major milestone
prompt_templates.prompt: Review all Memory Bank files for stale assumptions, missing decisions, unclear scope, and contradictions with the current codebase.

output_examples.input: Initialize a Memory Bank for a small web app.
output_examples.output: The assistant asks for project goals, core requirements, and scope. It then drafts concise Memory Bank documents for context, architecture, technology, active work, and progress.

output_examples.input: Update memory bank after adding authentication.
output_examples.output: The assistant updates the current focus, recent changes, completed work, known issues, and any new architecture decisions related to authentication.

output_examples.input: Resume work from the Memory Bank.
output_examples.output: The assistant reads the project documents, identifies the latest focus, reports blockers, and proposes the next implementation step.

best_practices: Review Memory Bank files before trusting them in a new session.
Update active context and progress after each significant change.
Keep entries concise, dated when useful, and tied to real project state.
anti_patterns: Do not store secrets, credentials, or private tokens in Memory Bank files.
Do not let stale notes override current source code or user instructions.
Do not use Memory Bank files as a replacement for tests, commits, or issue tracking.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It creates a structured memory-bank folder with Markdown files for project goals, context, architecture, technology, active work, and progress.

faq.question: Does it run background services?
faq.answer: No. The skill is documentation guidance and a static directory creation example.

faq.question: Can it work with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code?
faq.answer: Yes. The workflow is text-based and can guide Claude, Codex, and Claude Code sessions.

faq.question: What is the main security concern?
faq.answer: Persistent documentation can carry stale or injected instructions into later sessions if users do not review it.

faq.question: Should Memory Bank files contain secrets?
faq.answer: No. Store project context only. Keep credentials, tokens, and private keys out of these files.

faq.question: How often should the files be updated?
faq.answer: Update them after major decisions, completed work, important discoveries, and explicit memory update requests.
